one_liner: CMG up 3.3% on 2.96x volume (Selling Climax signal) with RSI at 61 approaching overbought — DCF shows 56% downside to $14.59; a premium brand but significantly overvalued at current levels.

news_catalyst.headline: Chipotle Bouncing on No Specific News — Premium QSR Valuation Under Scrutiny
news_catalyst.summary: CMG is up 3.3% today on 2.96x volume — the analysis flags this as a 'Selling Climax — possible reversal' from a recent low. No specific news catalyst. Chipotle's brand remains best-in-class in fast casual dining, but the DCF shows the stock trading at $33 vs. $14.59 intrinsic value (56% downside). The premium valuation reflects growth expectations that may be difficult to sustain in a consumer spending slowdown.

technical.summary: CMG bouncing toward resistance at $35.78 (only 7% above) on climactic volume. RSI at 61 approaching overbought. MACD in bullish crossover. The Selling Climax interpretation suggests this could be a short-term reversal point. Risk/reward is poor with only $2.44 to resistance vs. $4.40 to support.

valuation.summary: 56% DCF downside — even the bull case of $27.45 is 18% below current price. CMG is priced for exceptional growth that the 8.3% growth model can't justify. Would need to see 15%+ sustained growth to approach current market price.

key_strengths: Best-in-class QSR brand with digital ordering moat
Unit economics improving with throughput initiatives
fundamentals.summary: Grade C fundamentals — excellent business, poor value at current price. Chipotle is the kind of company you want to own at the right price. The right price is significantly lower than $33.

risks: 56% DCF downside — significantly overvalued on any reasonable growth assumption
Resistance only 7% above current price ($35.78) — asymmetric risk/reward
Consumer spending slowdown will impact discretionary dining
